title: Executive Order 9523—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the American Enka Corporation Located at or Near Enka, North Carolina

flags.justification: 'In such manner as he may deem necessary for the effective prosecution of the war' — broad, open discretion.
A concrete 60-day cap on possession is set.
Single purpose: seizure of this one company's plants.
Operation is required to be 'in accordance with the provisions of all applicable laws' — a real, if generic, constraint distinguishing this from sibling orders with no such qualifier.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
The 60-day cap is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap provides a real, checkable structural constraint.
A routine application of the established wartime plant-seizure template.
No other agency's mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
